purpose: Identifies the sequential pattern of disinflation and associated risk windows, particularly the mission accomplished trap during the Phase 2 shelter-to-services transition
components: Phase 1: Goods disinflation via supply chain normalization and base effects
Phase 2: Shelter/OER deceleration delivering 40-80bp mechanical headline CPI relief
Phase 3: Services ex-shelter resolution — the binding constraint, historically requiring demand destruction
Trap identification: 4/6 base rate for premature 'mission accomplished' during Phase 2
Exception condition: productivity growth >2.5% (1995 precedent)
current_assessment: Currently transitioning from Phase 1 to Phase 2. OER deceleration expected Q2-Q4 2026. Services ex-shelter at 3.5-4.0% with strong unit labor cost support. The trap window is the highest-conviction near-term risk across all agents.
limitations: Pattern-matched from only 6 episodes with researcher degrees of freedom in defining phases
Susceptible to hindsight bias without pre-registered predictions
Confidence 6/10 — useful heuristic, not a precision timing model
Cannot time factor rotations or sector trades at the specificity some agents attempt

purpose: Provides multi-dimensional similarity assessment across historical episodes rather than relying on any single analogue, acknowledging structural novelties that limit all analogues to <7.0/10 similarity
components: Seven-dimension scoring matrix for historical episode comparison
Structural novelty identification: mortgage lock-in (85%+ below 5%), >90% covenant-lite, AI investment cycle, simultaneous deglobalization + fiscal expansion at 6-7% GDP
Composite scoring weighted by dimension relevance
Explicit treatment of non-matching features as individually material
current_assessment: No single analogue scores above 7.0/10. The 1966-69 analogue downgraded from 6.5-7.0 to 5-6/10 after accounting for five material non-matches. Compound approach judged stronger than single-analogue reliance by pair_0 debate.
limitations: Dimension weights are subjective, creating an illusion of objectivity
Outcome distributions may be wider than any analogue framework suggests
At the point of compound structural novelty, analogue-based reasoning may be approaching unreliability
Does not produce actionable timing signals

purpose: Models the interaction effects of three concurrent vulnerabilities as a system that could produce unprecedented crisis sequencing through a delay-then-burst mechanism
components: Fed dovish asymmetry: premature easing creates macro conditions for stress
Covenant-lite delay: >90% covenant-lite with EBITDA addbacks delays default recognition 12-24 months
CLO OC mechanics: overcollateralization test failures cascade through structured credit
Interaction effect: delay-then-burst mechanism where opacity creates information gaps resolving suddenly
Analogues: LTCM, SVB — both involved opacity-driven sudden resolution
current_assessment: Each component individually documented at moderate-to-high confidence, but the three-way interaction is theorized rather than empirically observed. No historical precedent for this exact combination. Confidence 5/10 — valuable as monitoring framework, not actionable as positioning signal.
limitations: No historical precedent for the exact three-way interaction
Timing uncertainty is extreme
Each component's causal contribution is an identification problem
Counterfactual (behavior without one component) is unobservable

purpose: Evaluates whether contrarian positioning risk-reward is superior to consensus by comparing payoff ratios against positioning crowding, regardless of probability-weighted expected outcomes
components: Consensus positioning measurement: BofA Global FMS, CFTC data, breakeven levels
Payoff asymmetry calculation: 50-100bp Treasury rally (contrarian) vs. 10-20bp remaining return (consensus)
Payoff ratio: 3:1 to 5:1 favoring contrarian scenario
Falsification criteria: three consecutive core PCE ≤0.2% m/m, services ex-shelter <3.5% without unemployment >4.5%
Continuous positioning data monitoring requirement
current_assessment: The sticky-above-target consensus is the most crowded macro view. Even at 55-60% probability for consensus, the contrarian payoff ratio is 3:1 to 5:1. Identified as the single most actionable insight across all agents. Position sizing should reflect payoff asymmetry, not just probability-weighted expectations.
limitations: Requires continuous monitoring of positioning data
Payoff ratio estimates depend on current positioning levels which can shift
Does not specify optimal position size or structure
Contrarian view requires only that consensus is priced, not that inflation returns to 2%

purpose: Assesses wage-price spiral probability through conjunctive prerequisite analysis requiring simultaneous satisfaction of multiple independent conditions
components: Union density threshold
COLA coverage threshold
Sustained wage growth >5%
Expectations de-anchoring
Historical base rate: 2/~16 post-war cycles
Current conditions assessment: 0/3-4 prerequisites satisfied
current_assessment: Probability <10-15% under current conditions. 2021-23 natural experiment (2/3 conditions temporarily met, no spiral) provides genuine out-of-sample validation. Structural decline in union density and COLA coverage is secular.
limitations: Assumes the historical prerequisite set is complete
Novel labor market structures could create spiral risk through different channels
Conjunctive probability framework requires independence assumption across prerequisites

purpose: Models the reflexive loop where falling headline inflation loosens financial conditions, extending credit cycles while the underlying inflation regime remains unresolved
components: Trigger: headline CPI declines (typically shelter-driven)
Transmission: loosened financial conditions via rates expectations, credit spreads, equity valuations
Credit extension: credit cycle extended by 12-18 months beyond fundamentals
Resolution: delayed but amplified correction when underlying regime asserts
Exception condition: genuine productivity acceleration >2.5% validates the loosening ex post
current_assessment: 4/5 historical episodes (1967-68, 1984-85, 1995-96, 2005-06) show the pattern. The 1995-96 exception required productivity growth absent today. Directly challenges near-term credit repricing timing assumptions.
limitations: Small sample of 5 episodes
Exception condition (productivity) is hard to identify in real time
Does not specify the magnitude of the eventual correction
Interaction with covenant-lite and fiscal support is unprecedented

purpose: Identifies and corrects systematic overconfidence in the knowledge base on small-sample claims, providing calibration guidance for the entire framework
components: Systematic bias estimate: 1-2 points of overconfidence on claims derived from n≤6 episodes
Clopper-Pearson bounds for small-sample base rates
Fisher exact test for conditional probability claims (e.g., p≈0.10 on 1966-69 conditional base rates)
Correlated-error identification in additive decompositions
Confirmation bias detection: zero disinflationary scenarios after 47 iterations
current_assessment: The KB's central thesis is directionally supported but over-confidently calibrated. The systematic bias propagates through the entire framework. Highest-confidence findings are those supported by large cross-country evidence or contractual/arithmetic mechanisms.
limitations: Correction magnitude (1-2 points) is itself estimated
Does not identify which specific claims are most affected
Does not provide a replacement calibration methodology
May over-correct on claims with genuine structural underpinning
